Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: 2. Analyze the Standard P2P Process: Before looking at the data, I need to establish a baseline of what a normal or ideal P2P process looks like. Standard Sequence: 1. Requisition: Create Purchase Requisition (PR). 2. Approval: Approve PR (Internal control). 3. Ordering: Issue Purchase Order (PO). 4. Receipt: Receive Goods/Services (GRN - Goods Receipt Note). 5. Invoice: Vendor Invoice Received. 6. Matching: Match Invoice to PO (and often GRN). 7. Payment: Release Payment. 8. Closure: Close Case. Key Controls/Logic: Requisition must happen before PO. Approval must happen before PO (usually). PO must happen before Invoice and before Goods Receipt (usually, to ensure authorization). Goods Receipt usually happens before or around Invoice (to verify quantity/quality). Invoice Matching usually happens after Invoice and after PO (and often after Goods Receipt). Payment must happen after Matching (to ensure validity). Payment must happen before Case Close. Time gaps should make sense (eg, payment before invoice is impossible).

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: 3. Analyze Each Case Individually: Case 1001: Sequence: Create PR -> Approve PR -> Issue PO -> Receive Goods -> Invoice Received -> Match Invoice -> Release Payment -> Close Case. Check: PR before PO? Yes. Approve before PO? Yes. PO before Goods/Invoice? Yes. Match before Payment? Yes. Payment before Close? Yes. Verdict: Looks standard. No obvious anomalies.

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Case 1002: Sequence: Create PR -> Issue PO -> Invoice Received -> Release Payment -> Approve PR -> Receive Goods -> Match Invoice -> Close Case. Check: Issue PO before Approve PR? Yes. Major Issue: Payment happened before the Purchase Requisition was approved (02/03 07:15 vs 08:00). Even worse, Payment happened before Goods Received (02/04). Payment before Goods Received? Yes (02/03 vs 02/04). Payment before Invoice Match? Yes (02/03 vs 02/05). PO before Approval? Yes (08:40 vs 02/03 08:00). Verdict: Highly anomalous. Payment made before approval, before goods received, before matching. High fraud/risk risk.

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Case 1003: Sequence: Create PR -> Approve PR -> Issue PO -> Release Payment -> Invoice Received -> Receive Goods -> Match Invoice -> Close Case. Check: Payment before Invoice Received? Yes (02/08 vs 02/09). Payment before Goods Received? Yes (02/08 vs 02/10). Payment before Matching? Yes (02/08 vs 02/11). Verdict: Highly anomalous. Paying before receiving goods or invoice violates the Three-Way Match principle.

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Case 1004: Sequence: Create PR -> Approve PR -> Receive Goods -> Issue PO -> Invoice Received -> Match Invoice -> Release Payment -> Close Case. Check: Receive Goods before Issue PO? Yes (02/06 09:00 vs 02/06 09:15). Note: While the time gap is small (15 mins), logically, you generally shouldnt receive goods before there is an authorized PO. This is Blind Receiving or Maverick Buying. Verdict: Anomalous. Receiving before PO creates inventory/financial reconciliation issues and bypasses authorization.

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: 4. Synthesize Findings: Case 1001: Normal. Case 1002: Multiple severe anomalies (Payment before Approval, Payment before Goods, Payment before Match). Case 1003: Severe anomaly (Payment before Invoice/Goods). Case 1004: Moderate/Significant anomaly (Goods before PO).

Name: Backtracking - PE
Text: 6. Refine the Analysis (Self-Correction/Edge Cases): Case 1002: The timestamp for Approve Purchase Requisition is 02/03 08:00. The Release Payment was 02/03 07:15. So Payment happened before Approval. Also, Goods Received was 02/04. So Payment happened before Goods. Case 1003: Payment 02/08. Invoice 02/09. Goods 02/10. This is paying for nothing. Case 1004: Goods 09:00. PO 09:15. This is a post-dated PO or receiving without authorization. Case 1001: Just ensure I dont miss anything subtle. 02/02 Goods, 02/02 Invoice. 02/05 Match. 02/06 Payment. This is a bit slow (3 days to match), but not an anomaly in sequence.

Name: Validation - PE
Text: 7. Final Polish: Ensure the tone is professional and analytical. Highlight the specific business controls violated (Segregation of Duties, Authorization, Three-Way Match). (Self-Correction during drafting): Make sure to explicitly mention the Three-Way Match concept as its central to P2P controls.
